metadata.notes:
agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

The scenario's two Expected bullets are (1) the session appears in `ready` within 1s of agent launch and (2) the PID is bound within 1s. Both are directly observable through irrlicht's emitted event stream for the claudecode adapter. The daemon's process scanner runs `pgrep -x claude` (per `ExactName{Name: "claude"}` in `core/adapters/inbound/agents/claudecode/agent.go`), and on first detection it emits a `presession_created` row keyed by `proc-<PID>`. That pre-session is materialized in the `ready` state, so the spec's first bullet is satisfied independent of any transcript file existing on disk. The committed `claudecode/basic-turn/expected.jsonl` already anchors a `session_birth` phase (`expected_state: ready`) against recording start with `max_delay_ms: 1000`, confirming the timing budget is met in practice.

The second bullet — PID bound within 1s — is satisfied by the `pid_discovered` event emitted from `DiscoverPID` in `core/adapters/inbound/agents/claudecode/pid.go`. Because the pre-session row IS keyed by PID from inception (via `processlifecycle.DiscoverPIDByCWD` matching `ProcessName="claude"`), PID binding happens at the same scanner tick as session birth. The fixture's `pid_bind` phase (`kind: pid_discovered`, `max_delay_ms: 1000`) corroborates this. Both spec bullets therefore land cleanly inside irrlicht's observability surface, anchored to agent launch — not to transcript materialization.

## What irrlicht does NOT see (and why that's OK)

Irrlicht does not see the UUID-keyed session row at launch, because claudecode uses lazy transcript materialization — the JSONL file under `~/.claude/projects/<projHash>/<UUID>.jsonl` is not written until the first user message. The spec's "session appears in ready within 1s" is nonetheless satisfied because the daemon's pre-session (`proc-<PID>`) row covers the idle window between launch and first user input. The eventual `transcript_new` event reconciles the pre-session into the UUID session once the transcript appears. This is the feature-invisible-but-spec-compliant pattern: the user-visible row exists on time even though the underlying file does not.
caveats: Claudecode uses lazy transcript materialization: the UUID-keyed session row only appears after the first user message lands. The spec's 'within 1s of agent launch' bullet is satisfied by the pre-session (`proc-<PID>`) row, not the UUID session — a known divergence between the user's mental model and the matrix verdict.

Both timing bullets are anchored to the daemon's process-scan tick, so a scanner interval longer than ~1s would violate the spec; the current configuration and basic-turn fixture confirm the budget is met in practice.

sources.note: Confirmed Identity.Name=claude-code, Source variant FilesUnderRoot with transcriptsDir() under ~/.claude/projects/, and ExactName{Name: "claude"} process matcher.
Confirmed DiscoverPID prefers ~/.claude/sessions/<UUID>.json metadata and falls back to DiscoverPIDByCWD matching ProcessName=claude — feeding the pid_discovered event.

Committed fixture asserts session_birth (expected_state: ready, max_delay_ms: 1000) and pid_bind (kind: pid_discovered, max_delay_ms: 1000) phases anchored to recording start — the same two bullets this scenario's spec asserts.

preconditions: claude CLI on PATH and authenticated (subscription or API key)
irrlichd is recording — either run-cell.sh in isolated mode, or --attach against a running daemon started with --record
no other claudecode session uses the same UUID
setup: Generate a fresh lowercased session UUID (run-cell.sh does this via uuidgen | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')
Create an empty cwd under <staging>/cwd/ (claude prompts for trust on first encounter — the driver auto-accepts)

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ok

verify: events.jsonl contains a presession_created event for proc-<PID> within 1000 ms of the tmux new-session timestamp
events.jsonl contains a state_transition to ready for proc-<PID> within 100 ms of presession_created (pre-session immediately in ready)
events.jsonl contains a pid_discovered event for proc-<PID> within 1000 ms
events.jsonl contains a transcript_new event for the UUID session within 500 ms of the first send (claudecode creates the transcript on first input)
events.jsonl contains a state_transition to ready for the UUID session within 500 ms of transcript_new
events.jsonl contains a pid_discovered event for the UUID session (PID re-bound to the UUID at handoff)
events.jsonl contains a presession_removed event for proc-<PID> at handoff
transcript.jsonl contains exactly 1 user line and 1 assistant line
